note: Curated by the VP Legislation, by hand. The nightly fetch never touches this file — parent cards are written consequences, not scraped headlines. Every card needs: kid_impact (one sentence, what changes for a student/family), owner (who actually decides), and at most one action. Give any dated coming_up card an 'until' (YYYY-MM-DD): the page hides it the day after, so a past meeting can never sit there saying 'coming up'. If an item can't be phrased as a consequence with an owner, it stays in the VP Desk. The watchlist is VP-Desk-only: known scheduled obligations with a date they're expected by — the page flags anything past due that isn't marked done, so a blank where a report should be never goes unnoticed.

coming_up.location: Arcadia Education Center Board Room, 150 S 3rd Ave, Arcadia, CA 91006
coming_up.title: AUSD Board of Education meets Tuesday, July 14
coming_up.kid_impact: Summer meetings are where next school year gets decided — expect the district's required smartphone policy, budget items, and facilities plans to move here before anything reaches classrooms in August.
coming_up.time_sensitivity: Agenda posts on Simbli a few days before the meeting

in_effect.title: New phone rules are coming when school starts
in_effect.kid_impact: A state law now requires Arcadia USD to have a policy limiting student smartphone use during school hours. Expect specific phone rules from the district before the first day in August. Emergencies, medical needs, and IEP/504 plans are exempt by law.
in_effect.owner: Arcadia USD board sets the policy; Holly Avenue implements it
in_effect.time_sensitivity: In effect July 1 — details arrive before school starts

in_effect.title: Stronger abuse-prevention rules at every school
in_effect.kid_impact: Schools must now run expanded background checks on job applicants, train staff and students to recognize signs of grooming or abuse, and post their safe-environment policy where parents can see it.
in_effect.owner: Arcadia USD; Holly Avenue must post its policy
in_effect.law_label: SB 848 — Pupil safety: employee misconduct, abuse prevention

action.label: Ask the office where the policy is posted

in_effect.title: Every school must offer an all-gender restroom
in_effect.kid_impact: Campuses must provide at least one unlocked, signed all-gender restroom during school hours — often by re-signing an existing single-stall restroom. A facilities change; nothing for families to do.

actions.label: Skim the next board agenda
actions.detail: Board decisions land on Simbli before each meeting. Ten minutes of skimming beats a month of rumors.

actions.label: Email the AUSD board
actions.detail: Public comment doesn't require attending. Board members read email about agenda items, especially before a vote.

actions.label: Bring an issue to the PTA
actions.detail: Pickup chaos, confusing phone rules, 'nobody told us' — if it has an owner and a next decision point, the PTA can route it. Talk to any board member.

watchlist.why: State law required board adoption by June 30. Verify it happened in the June minutes, then mark done — this is where next year's staffing and program money got set. (Checked Jul 2: the district's LCFF page still posts only the 2025-26 LCAP, so adoption can't be confirmed from open sources — the June 23 minutes on Simbli are the check.)
The district agenda page still shows last year. When the new schedule posts, the nightly fetch auto-resumes meeting cards; until then, Coming Up is hand-fed.
Required by state law; parents will ask about it the first week of school. Watch board agendas for the policy item, then turn it into a parent card.
State test scores land in the fall; district usually comments. Expect parent questions comparing Holly Avenue to district and state.
Every December the board elects its president and sets the year. Committee assignments here decide who to email about what.
The School Accountability Report Card is due by February 1 each year — the one document that answers most 'how is our school doing' questions with receipts.
